Line seven prints status code and JSON when the response is successful. Run this after Ansible apply to cross-check structured state against CLI verification. A four oh four may mean VLAN one hundred was not created on that platform path — investigate before closing the lab.
slides.title: RESTCONF VLAN Validation
slides.code:
import requests; from requests.auth import HTTPBasicAuth
BASE = "https://192.168.1.10/restconf/data"
HEADERS = {"Accept": "application/yang-data+json"}
AUTH = HTTPBasicAuth("admin", "lab-password")
url = f"{BASE}/Cisco-IOS-XE-native:native/vlan/vlan-list=100"
r = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, auth=AUTH, verify=False)
if r.ok: print(r.status_code, r.json())

slides.name: ansible-capstone-check-mode
slides.script: Line one runs ansible-playbook against capstone inventory and the branch_site playbook with minus minus check. Ansible reports changed, ok, and skipped per task without applying configuration. Read output on both branch-sw1 and branch-sw2 before removing check mode. Add minus minus limit branch-sw1 when testing one switch first. If check mode shows unexpected interface changes, fix the playbook YAML before apply — the same discipline module four emphasized.
slides.title: Dry Run the Branch Playbook
slides.code: ansible-playbook -i capstone/inventory/branch.yml capstone/playbooks/branch_site.yml --check

slides.name: run-capstone-pipeline
slides.script: Run the capstone pipeline in order with your virtual environment active. Line one dry-runs the branch playbook with check mode. Line two applies the playbook without check. Line three runs Python verification against both switches. Line four runs RESTCONF validation on switch one — skip if HTTPS is unavailable in your lab. Archive the terminal output with the Git commit hash you applied. That record is your capstone evidence: dry run, apply, CLI verify, API verify.
slides.title: Execute the Capstone Pipeline
slides.code:
ansible-playbook -i capstone/inventory/branch.yml capstone/playbooks/branch_site.yml --check
ansible-playbook -i capstone/inventory/branch.yml capstone/playbooks/branch_site.yml
python3 capstone/scripts/verify_branch.py
python3 capstone/scripts/restconf_validate.py

slides.name: story-beat-common-mistake-capstone
slides.script: The capstone mistake that ends careers early is running apply on the full inventory before check mode on one switch. A typo in the OSPF network statement propagates to every branch device in minutes. Another failure is verifying only switch one while switch two never received the playbook because of a stale IP in inventory. Capstone discipline means limit during test, check mode always, verify every host you changed, and RESTCONF or show commands before you tell your manager the job is done. Automation scales your mistakes as fast as your successes.
slides.title: Never Skip Limit and Check

slides.name: certification-alignment-full-course
slides.script: The full course maps to certification objectives one point one through one point six. Module four and this capstone cover Ansible construction for VLANs, interfaces, OSPF, and ACLs — objective one point one. Module four introduced Terraform plan selection — one point two. Module three and RESTCONF validation here address RESTCONF with YANG and REST API error handling — one point three and one point six. Module two and Python verification cover Python automation — one point four. Tool matrix slides across modules four and five address approach selection — one point five. Practice explaining each objective with a file from automation_project, not abstract definitions alone.
slides.title: Full Course Exam Alignment

slides.name: course-complete-enterprise-automation
slides.script: You began this course typing show and configure commands on one device. You finish it with Git-versioned inventory, Ansible branch playbooks, Python verification, RESTCONF reads, check mode habit, and a capstone pipeline that ties CCNA configuration tasks to modern automation delivery. That is the skill employers and certification scenarios test — not memorizing a single vendor GUI. Keep automation_project, refine it on lab gear, document what you run, and never apply unchecked playbooks to production. You are ready to practice exam scenarios and extend this branch workflow to your own designs.
slides.title: Course Complete — Automate with Discipline
points: Capstone branch pipeline in your lab
